test_description: Fit and subtract the monopole and dipole on the unmasked pixels, then compute masked pseudo-harmonic coefficients in the ecliptic coordinate frame for 2 <= l <= 20 using the same mask weights for data and simulations. Return the single scalar F = sum_l |a_l0|^2 / sum_l [|a_l0|^2 + 2 sum_{m=1}^l |a_lm|^2], i.e. the fraction of low-l power in m=0 zonal modes about the ecliptic poles.
test_hypothesis: The Planck CMB map has an anomalously high fraction of low-l temperature power concentrated in ecliptic m=0 zonal modes compared with simulations.
justificaton: The statistic F is constructed so that larger values mean more low-l power lies in ecliptic zonal modes. The test specifically targets excess concentration about the ecliptic poles, and the code identifies the upper tail as “unusually high” or “excess” ecliptic zonal power.

custom_summary.test_description: Low-l Ecliptic Zonal Power Fraction: fit and subtract the monopole and dipole on unmasked pixels, zero-fill masked pixels, compute low-l masked pseudo-harmonic power for 2<=l<=20, and return the fraction in ecliptic m=0 zonal modes.
custom_summary.statistic: F = sum_{l=2}^{20} |a_l0^E|^2 / sum_{l=2}^{20} (|a_l0^E|^2 + 2 sum_{m=1}^l |a_lm^E|^2) after masked monopole/dipole subtraction.
custom_summary.coordinate_frame: Ecliptic zonal axis. Input HEALPix maps are treated as Galactic/RING; Y_l0 is evaluated about the ecliptic pole.
custom_summary.mask_handling: Only finite pixels enter the monopole/dipole fit. The cut-sky map is then zero-filled on masked pixels before harmonic analysis, giving the same binary mask weights for data and simulations.
custom_summary.tail_of_interest: Upper tail indicates unusually high low-l power in ecliptic m=0 zonal modes; lower tail indicates a zonal deficit.

plot_spec.title: Low-l ecliptic zonal power fraction
plot_spec.xlabel: F: fraction of l=2..20 pseudo-power in ecliptic m=0 modes
plot_spec.caption: Planck is compared with simulations using identical masking, monopole/dipole subtraction, and pseudo-harmonic weighting.

custom_summary.interpretation: Planck is not in the 5% upper or lower tail of the simulation distribution for this ecliptic zonal power fraction.

test_summary.Description: After monopole/dipole subtraction on unmasked pixels, the test computes the masked pseudo-harmonic fraction of low-l temperature power in ecliptic m=0 modes for 2<=l<=20.
test_summary.Results: Planck gives F=0.1094, compared with simulations with mean 0.0993, median 0.0893, and std 0.0528. It lies at the 65.8th percentile; 342/1000 simulations have larger F. The one-tailed upper p-value is 0.342, about 0.41 sigma.
test_summary.Interpretation: Planck has a slightly above-average ecliptic zonal power fraction, but this is common in the LCDM simulation ensemble and is far from the 5% upper-tail anomaly threshold.
test_summary.Comparison to literature: No directly comparable published test of this exact l=2..20 masked ecliptic m=0 power-fraction statistic was identified. Related low-l ecliptic-alignment studies, such as Weeks (2004) and Abramo et al. (2006), use multipole-vector/alignment statistics rather than this zonal power fraction.
